title: NPotFixer
description: Makes your modded Minecraft look the way it should - no more broken textures from weird image sizes.
body:
# 🎨 NPotFixer

![Modrinth Downloads](https://img.shields.io/modrinth/dt/NkzZ1fXl?color=blue&label=Downloads&style=flat-square)
![Mod Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-1.0.0-green?style=flat-square)
![Minecraft](https://img.shields.io/badge/mc-1.20.1+-orange?style=flat-square)
![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-All%20rights%20reserved-red?style=flat-square)

> Fixes broken, blurry textures in modded Minecraft caused by NPOT (Non-Power-Of-Two) images.  
> Drop it in. Play. Done. 👊

---

## 🔧 What It Does

- ✅ Fixes blurry or broken textures caused by weird image sizes (like 37×19)
- ✅ Makes far-distance sprites look sharp again
- ✅ Drop-in ready - zero configuration
- ✅ Great for modpacks, custom textures, and Sodium users

---

## 🤔 Why It Matters

Many Minecraft mods use custom textures that don’t follow the **power-of-two (POT)** rule.  
This breaks mipmaps and causes artifacts, especially when viewing from a distance.

**NPotFixer** automatically resizes and corrects these textures **without touching the original files.**

> 📚 Learn more:
> - [Mipmap on Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mipmap)
> - [NPOT Textures - Soft8Soft Wiki](https://www.soft8soft.com/wiki/index.php/NPOT_Textures)

---

## 🧩 How to Use

1. Download the latest `.jar` from [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/mod/npotfixer)
2. Drop it into your `mods/` folder
3. Launch the game - and enjoy flawless textures 🎮

---
